expectations.text: Every input assumption has a Named Range with Inp_ prefix (e.g., Inp_Customers_Y1, Inp_Price_Monthly, Inp_Customer_Growth, Inp_Price_Increase, Inp_Churn_Monthly)
expectations.evidence: Named ranges use lowercase descriptive names without Inp_ prefix: starting_customers, starting_monthly_price, annual_customer_growth_rate, annual_price_increase_rate, monthly_churn_rate. Does not follow IDFA Inp_ convention.

expectations.text: All calculation formulas reference Named Ranges only — zero cell-coordinate references like A1, B2, $C$3
expectations.evidence: All formulas in the specification reference named ranges: e.g., 'monthly_price_Y1 = starting_monthly_price', 'eom_customers_Y1 = Month 12 EoM Customers'. No cell-coordinate references like A1 or $C$3 appear in calculation formulas.

expectations.text: The model separates into three layers: Assumptions (inputs only), Calculations (logic only), Output (presentation only)
expectations.evidence: The workbook has separate sheets: Assumptions, Customer_Model, Pricing_Model, Revenue_Model, SaaS_Metrics, Summary_Dashboard. While more than 3 sheets, it follows an inputs-first, calculations-middle, dashboard-last architecture.

expectations.text: Revenue calculations correctly account for churn reducing the customer base
expectations.evidence: Monthly detail formulas show 'Churned Customers This Month = BoM_Customers * monthly_churn_rate' and 'EoM Customers = BoM + New - Churned'. The model explicitly notes the tension between 20% growth and 2% monthly churn.

expectations.text: The specification includes both MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) and ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) as Named Ranges
expectations.evidence: Revenue Model section includes total_MRR_start_Y[n], total_MRR_end_Y[n], avg_MRR_Y[n], ARR_start_Y[n], ARR_end_Y[n] as Named Ranges.

expectations.text: Each formula is readable as a plain-English business rule without needing to trace cell references
expectations.evidence: Formulas use descriptive named ranges like 'starting_customers', 'monthly_churn_rate' making them readable. Business rules are described in plain language sections.

expectations.text: Year-over-year naming convention uses _Y1, _Y2 etc. dimension suffixes consistently
expectations.evidence: Named ranges consistently use _Y[n] suffixes: bom_customers_Y1-Y5, monthly_price_Y1-Y5, annual_revenue_Y1-Y5, etc. Complete Named Range Registry shows 161 ranges following this convention.
